statement.markdown: Write a function to validate if all HTML tags in a given string are properly closed. A tag is properly closed if it has the same name as its corresponding opening tag and in the correct order.
statement.inputFormat: Input is a string containing HTML tags
statement.outputFormat: Return true if all tags are properly closed, false otherwise
statement.notes: Time complexity should be O(n), where n is the length of the input string

examples.explanation: Body tag is not properly closed

hints.text: Use a stack data structure to keep track of opening tags

editorial.title: Editorial: HTML Tag Validation
editorial.intuition: The idea is to push opening tags onto a stack and pop from the stack when a closing tag is encountered. If a closing tag is not found in the stack, return false.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach: parse the entire string and check for each tag
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a stack to keep track of opening tags
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Correctness can be proved by analyzing the stack operations
